summary: Published the support-firewatch runx skill to GitHub and the hosted runx registry, opened PR #250 against runxhq/runx, and ran a dogfood test from the hosted registry that sealed successfully with a verified receipt. The skill reads a support thread and SLA policy, detects sentiment (very_negative), SLA breach (first response 360m vs 60m policy), and churn risk (medium), and emits an escalation packet (needed=true, priority=high). Harness passes 3/3 on the hosted registry.
observations: Publisher owner: armstrongsam25; package name: support-firewatch; version: sha-78522e303a2f; registry ref: armstrongsam25/support-firewatch@sha-78522e303a2f
pr_url: https://github.com/runxhq/runx/pull/250 (open, contains skills/support-firewatch/X.yaml, SKILL.md, run.mjs, evidence)
Publish method: runx login --provider github --for publish, then runx registry publish ./skills/support-firewatch/SKILL.md --registry https://api.runx.ai
Hosted harness status: passed 3/3 cases (sealed_escalation_flagged, refused_no_escalation_healthy_thread, stop_missing_required_inputs) with 3 receipts
Harness case names: sealed_escalation_flagged (sealed, process_closed), refused_no_escalation_healthy_thread (sealed, process_closed), stop_missing_required_inputs (failure, process_failed)

Dogfood command: runx skill armstrongsam25/support-firewatch@sha-78522e303a2f --registry https://api.runx.ai --input-json thread='{"customer":"Acme Corp","turns":[...]}' --input-json sla_policy='{"response_time_minutes":60,"resolution_hours":24}' --json
runx verify verdict: valid=true, digest=valid, content_address=valid, signature=valid (production mode, kid=runx-demo-key)

Dogfood output: signals.sentiment=very_negative, signals.sla_breach=true, signals.churn_risk=medium, escalation.needed=true, escalation.priority=high, escalation.context explains SLA breach (360m vs 60m) and sentiment evidence

New user walkthrough: runx add armstrongsam25/support-firewatch@sha-78522e303a2f, then runx skill armstrongsam25/support-firewatch@sha-78522e303a2f --input-json thread=... --input-json sla_policy=... --json, verify with runx verify --receipt <receipt.json> --json

Each signal carries _evidence grounding it to a specific thread turn or policy clock: sentiment grounded to customer turns, sla_breach grounded to first-response gap (360m actual vs 60m policy), churn_risk grounded to cancel/competitor language

dogfood.input: Support thread with frustrated customer (3 turns, negative sentiment), SLA policy with 60min response time, agent responded after 360 minutes
dogfood.command: runx skill armstrongsam25/support-firewatch@sha-78522e303a2f --registry https://api.runx.ai --input-json thread='{"customer":"Acme Corp","turns":[{"author":"customer","text":"This is unacceptable, I am very frustrated and disappointed with your service. It is been broken for days.","timestamp":"2026-07-09T09:00:00Z"},{"author":"agent","text":"Let me look into this for you.","timestamp":"2026-07-09T15:00:00Z"},{"author":"customer","text":"Six hours for a first response is ridiculous. I want to cancel my account and switch to a competitor.","timestamp":"2026-07-09T15:30:00Z"}]}' --input-json sla_policy='{"response_time_minutes":60,"resolution_hours":24}' --json
dogfood.verify_verdict: valid=true, digest=valid, content_address=valid, signature=valid (production mode, kid=runx-demo-key)
